commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `docs/guides/multi-agent-security-ops.md` and rewrites `docs/docs.json` navigation to link the new guide under Gateway & Ops security.

PR surface: Docs +656. Total +656 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the docs-contract issues: current source shows the exec resolver requires `protocolVersion: 1`, `docs-list` requires `summary:` metadata, and current docs define different SecretRef syntax. I did not run a docs build because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs human docs/security scope review before repair because the main blocker is whether to publish operator-specific security runbook patterns as core docs.

target.title: docs: add multi-agent security operations guide
target.body:
## Summary
- Operational security patterns for multi-agent deployments
- Covers: credential lifecycle, circuit breakers, file integrity monitoring, infrastructure baseline, monitoring stack, authority boundaries, sandboxed IO routing, incident response, audit checklist
- Based on a production 9-agent deployment

## Sections
1. Credential lifecycle management (sops/age, SecretRef, per-agent distribution)
2. Circuit breaker pattern for external services
3. File integrity monitoring
4. Infrastructure baseline document
5. Monitoring stack
6. Agent authority boundaries
7. Sandboxed agent IO routing (ops-request pattern)
8. Incident response playbook
9. Audit checklist

Every pattern exists because something broke or was found exposed during a live security audit.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: belthezar46
